name: SecurityClaw
description: A modular, skill-based autonomous Security Operations Center (SOC) agent that monitors OpenSearch/Elasticsearch data, builds RAG-based behavioral memory, and validates real-time anomalies using LLMs.

commit.message:
Enhance query repair, field discovery, and routing

Multiple improvements across query repair, field discovery, memory, and chat routing:

- Config: increased default llm.max_tokens to 4096 and added memory settings for query_repair limits.
- core/query_builder: richer field classification (directional source/destination IP buckets, country, geo fields) and helper to classify IP fields; better nested-field handling.
- core/query_repair: added structural Python fixes (move misplaced size, strip placeholder @timestamp clauses), date-like string detection to avoid incorrect conversions, repeated-failure detection to abort identical errors, and deduplication of LLM-supplied fixes.
- core/query_repair_memory: made memory limits configurable via Config, added compaction to bound repairs/field types, trimmed field_types on insert, and compact on load/save.
- skills/chat_router: added prompt templates, prompt loading/rendering helpers, stronger routing rules (prevent geoip_lookup misuse, auto-queue opensearch_querier after fields_querier, remove unrequested threat_analyst enrichment), improved entity extraction and contextual threat follow-ups.

These changes harden query handling, reduce noisy/duplicate repair attempts, improve schema discovery, and make chat routing more accurate and context aware. Tests were updated accordingly.

Remove OpenAI support; Ollama-only provider

Remove OpenAI-related code, docs, and dependencies and standardize on Ollama as the sole LLM provider. Updates include: remove OPENAI_API_KEY from .env.example and examples, remove openai package from Pipfile/requirements, simplify onboarding to only prompt for Ollama settings and stop writing OpenAI env vars, eliminate OpenAI fallback and provider code in core/llm_provider.py and core/config.py, update web/api server to stop exposing OPENAI_API_KEY, and adjust README/ONBOARDING/config examples to reflect Ollama-only usage. This consolidates LLM handling to Ollama and removes API-key handling and OpenAI SDK usage across the project.
Update README.md

Clean unused import and update onboarding

Remove an unused Click Confirm import from core/skill_onboarding.py to avoid an unnecessary dependency. Update the onboarding completion message in main.py to show the new CLI entrypoints (python main.py chat and python main.py service) instead of the old python main.py run guidance.

Load config from example or use defaults

Attempt to read config.yaml; if missing, load config.yaml.example as a template. If neither file exists, populate a minimal default config structure (agent, scheduler, db, llm, rag) to ensure defaults are available at runtime. Keeps database credentials out of config (expected in .env) and preserves subsequent update of the db provider.

Use nomic-embed-text:latest for embeddings

Replace references to the lightweight embed model 'tinyllama' with 'nomic-embed-text:latest' across the project. Updated ONBOARDING.md and README.md examples and setup instructions, adjusted the core/llm_provider.py comment to mention the new embed model, and changed the default embed model prompt and config in main.py so docs, examples, and defaults are consistent.

Increase per-verdict summary length limits

Raise per_verdict_limit in _append_threat_intel_summary from 320/220 to 600/350 so single and multi-verdict summaries can include more context. This change increases the truncate thresholds for threat intel summaries (still iterates up to the first 3 verdicts) to reduce information loss when generating responses.

Prefer fields_querier for natural-language searches

Add heuristics and recovery to prefer field-discovery for NL log searches and improve result-aware orchestration. Introduces _prefer_field_discovery_for_natural_language_search and _apply_result_aware_recovery, auto-queues threat_analyst when reputation is requested and opensearch has evidence, and prevents finalizing when threat intel is missing. Enhances supervisor flow to dedupe/ban duplicate skill runs and to try recovery plans. Improves opensearch_querier logging, validation metadata, and reflection length; includes validation flags in returned results. Improves entity extraction and opensearch response formatting to handle nested fields (source/destination.ip, geoip, ports) and better port extraction. Adds tests covering NL port search routing and explicit-field behavior.

Increase default query result size to 200

Raise the default `size` parameter for search/query functions from 50 (and one instance of 100) to 200 to return more results by default. Updated function signatures and call sites across: core/db_connector.py (BaseDBConnector/OpenSearchConnector), core/query_repair.py (IntelligentQueryRepair.repair_and_retry), and skills/opensearch_querier/logic.py (search execution, run(), recovery logic, and explicit query). Also includes an updated web screenshot image (img/web-screenshot.png). This change aims to surface more results for searches and improve recovery/repair behavior when querying.

Create SECURITY.md for security policy

Added a security policy document outlining supported versions and vulnerability reporting.

commit.message:
Add Docker onboarding, compose, and web UI image

Add a Docker-based onboarding flow and docker-compose setup: new onboard-docker.sh to detect/extract Ollama settings (converts localhost → host.docker.internal, checks reachability, optionally runs Python onboarding) and start docker-compose; new docker-compose.yml to run the Python service and Vite web frontend with relevant env var wiring and port mappings. Also add a web UI screenshot (img/web-screenshot.png) and document the Docker Quick Start in ONBOARDING.md and README.md. Remove sentence-transformers from requirements.txt.

Enhance README with setup, web UI & embed model

Expand README onboarding and add web interface docs; update example config to use nomic embed model. Changes include: updated prerequisites (Python, Git, OpenSearch/Elasticsearch versions, RAM/disk recommendations), clarified Ollama setup and pull of nomic-embed-text, reworked installation into clone/venv/activate/install steps with verification, added detailed Web Interface section (startup, endpoints, features, access, headless example, troubleshooting), and updated config.yaml.example to set ollama_embed_model to nomic-embed-text:latest. These edits improve developer onboarding and clarify running the web server and embedding configuration.

Secure API endpoints and update docs

Update documentation and harden web API security. Docs: refresh ONBOARDING.md and README.md to reflect new/renamed skills, schedules, publication notes, CLI/service commands, and a skills status table (e.g. fields_baseliner, anomaly_triage, threat_analyst, opensearch_querier, forensic_examiner, etc.). API: tighten CORS to specific local origins, restrict methods and headers, disable allow_credentials; remove deprecated /api/situation endpoint; add input validation and path traversal protections for conversation deletion and skill save endpoints (validate conversation_id and skill_name, resolve paths and enforce directory bounds); validate manifest YAML before writing. These changes improve security and clarify operational guidance.

Switch working memory to compact JSON store

Replace the human-readable SITUATION.md memory with a bounded, structured JSON store (data/agent_memory.json) and refactor memory APIs accordingly. core/memory.py was rewritten to persist a versioned JSON store with size limits, thread-safety, markdown rendering for snapshots, and backwards-compatible parsing of legacy markdown. Runner and main were updated to use the new memory path/API (memory_path), improve lifecycle management (start/stop, signal registration, manual skill handling), and add memory entries for start/shutdown. CLI gained web-related commands (service, web-build, web-dev) and the status command now prints the compact memory snapshot. Documentation and examples (README, ONBOARDING, config.yaml.example) were updated to reference the new memory file; SKILL_ONBOARDING.md and multiple conversation fixtures were removed. Added a minimal web UI/API under web/ and FastAPI/uvicorn to requirements. Also: small skill and chat_router fixes (anomaly_triage schedule metadata removed; conversation listing improved).

Add dynamic skill onboarding; rename anomaly_watcher

Introduce a dynamic skill variable onboarding system and integrate it into the onboarding/chat flows. Adds core/skill_onboarding.py (discovery, prompting, state tracking, .env write), SKILL_ONBOARDING.md docs, and tests/tests_skill_onboarding.py. Wire onboarding into main.py (onboard step and first-chat check). Add required_env_vars to threat_analyst and geoip_lookup manifests so skill-specific secrets (MaxMind, AbuseIPDB, VirusTotal, Talos, etc.) are discovered and optionally saved. Rename anomaly_watcher -> anomaly_triage across skills, docs, tests and update chat_router logic to use dynamic available skills and updated fallback messaging. Update README and ONBOARDING.md to document the new onboarding phases and skill variable behavior.

Use skill-declared schedules, remove defaults

Remove the hardcoded DEFAULT_INTERVALS mapping and make Runner rely on scheduling metadata provided by skills (schedule_interval_seconds or schedule_cron_expr). When registering jobs, Runner now prefers skill.schedule_interval_seconds and falls back to the configurable scheduler. The scheduler preview was updated to display cron expressions, explicit intervals (every Ns), or "manual (on-demand)" for interactive skills. This decouples core from known skill names and lets skills declare their own scheduling in instruction front-matter.

Move reputation_intel and update baseline handling

Rename core/reputation_intel.py -> skills/threat_analyst/reputation_intel.py and update all imports and tests to reference the new module path. Change _analyze_finding to omit the Baseline Context section when RAG returns the sentinel "_No relevant context found._" to avoid misleading the LLM. Add tests (tests/test_threat_analyst_baseline_context.py) to verify baseline context is excluded when empty and included when relevant, and update existing tests to patch/import the moved reputation_intel module.

Add cron scheduling; GeoIP & OS fallback

Introduce cron-based scheduling and improve GeoIP and OpenSearch robustness. SkillLoader now extracts schedule_cron_expr and logs schedules; Runner supports cron registrations and prints cron schedules in the skill table; CLI list output shows cron expressions. GeoIP: example config annotated with download/update metadata, instruction updated to run Tue/Fri at 02:00 UTC, logic enhanced to extract IPs from previous opensearch results, perform multi-IP lookups, and prefer MaxMind API endpoint (with docs suggesting geoipupdate). Chat router: prioritization logic updated to promote opensearch_querier when field discovery is already present and to pass current_results through orchestration. OpenSearch querier: add heuristic extractors for ports and countries, fallback heuristic planner when LLM planning fails, a simplified LLM retry prompt, and improved recovery of follow-up queries (ports/protocols/geolocation) from prior results/conversation. Formatting updates: opensearch and geoip response formatting extended to include countries and multi-IP renderings. Tests updated and new tests added to cover cron/integration behaviors, geoip follow-ups, and opensearch LLM fallback paths.

Add MaxMind GeoIP lookup skill with weekly refresh

- add geoip_lookup skill for local MaxMind city/state/country enrichment
- auto-download GeoLite2-City MMDB on first use when missing
- refresh stale MMDB once per week via scheduled skill interval
- add GeoIP config/env support and dependency entries
- add deterministic chat formatting for geoip lookup responses
- add tests covering download, refresh, lookup, and formatter behavior

Fix follow-up IP country lookups in chat supervisor

- recover IP search terms from prior results/history for referential follow-up questions
- pass same-step skill results to later skills in execute_skill_workflow
- improve OpenSearch response formatting for flat geoip/source/destination fields
- add regression tests for follow-up context recovery and same-step result sharing

This fixes cases like 'What countries are these IPs from?' after an alert/IP summary,
where the LLM produced no explicit search criteria and the supervisor stopped early.

Fix CI test failures: handle missing config.yaml and update test assertions

Changes:
- core/config.py: Added graceful fallback to config.yaml.example when config.yaml missing
  * Useful for CI environments where config.yaml doesn't exist
  * Falls back automatically without breaking
  * Added logging to warn users to create proper config.yaml for production

- tests/test_skills.py: Fixed test_run_stores_rag_document assertions
  * Removed incorrect assertion for non-existent 'baseline_doc_id' key
  * Updated to check for actual returned keys: records_processed, networks_analyzed,
    documents_stored, identifier_field, identifiers
  * Test now validates correct response structure

- .github/workflows/tests.yml: Added config.yaml setup step
  * Copies config.yaml.example to config.yaml before running tests
  * Provides dual fallback strategy for CI compatibility

All 295 tests pass locally and ready for GitHub Actions CI

Add GitHub Actions workflow for automated tests on commits

- Runs pytest with coverage on every push and pull request
- Supports Python 3.11 (primary target)
- Uploads coverage reports to Codecov for tracking coverage trends
- Fails fast on syntax errors but doesn't fail CI on low coverage
- Automatically caches pip dependencies for faster runs

Security audit: Git history scanned for credential patterns
- Result: No real credentials found in tracked files or history
- All detected patterns are placeholder examples (admin, sk-..., angle brackets)
- config.yaml and SITUATION.md properly removed from history via filter-branch (commit 00df709)
- .env file correctly git-ignored, never appears in history
- safe to push to public repository

Add intelligent query repair + docs & test updates

Introduce an intelligent query-repair subsystem and related plumbing, plus documentation and test/tooling updates.

Major changes:
- Add core/query_repair.py and core/query_repair_memory.py: Python-level fixes, LLM-driven repair prompts, retry loop (IntelligentQueryRepair) and persistent memory of successful fixes/field types.
- Update core/db_connector.py: add _short_json helper, QueryMalformedException, and change search() to surface malformed-query errors (raise QueryMalformedException) and include hit _id in returned documents.
- Add core/skill_manifest.py (manifest loader stub) and adjust core/runner.py default intervals (add fields_baseliner and mark several queriers as on-demand/None).
- Improve query_builder fallback to read data/fields_rag.json as a fast path for field discovery.
- Docs and onboarding: switch example commands to .venv/bin/python, add Quick Ollama setup and example models (qwen2.5:7b-instruct-q4_K_M / tinyllama), sanitize config examples and .env placeholders; README and ONBOARDING.md updated accordingly.
- Tooling/test updates: add pytest-cov to Pipfile, update .gitignore for coverage/htmlcov and data, and add many new/updated skills and tests to support the new repair/skill flows.

These changes enable recoverable query execution with learnable repairs, improve skill manifest support, and align examples/tests for local Ollama-based development.

Add query_builder and opensearch_querier

Introduce a centralized core.query_builder module (field discovery and reusable query builders) and a new opensearch_querier skill that plans and executes OpenSearch queries via the LLM. Add resilient search handling in core/db_connector (retry + simplified fallback queries for 400/query errors). Update network_baseliner and rag_querier to use discovered field mappings and data-agnostic extraction, and refine chat_router routing instructions. Also add instruction doc for opensearch_querier and new/updated tests for query planning and conversation context.

Add reputation intel & forensic examiner skill

Introduce external reputation intelligence and a forensic timeline skill, and harden RAG/LLM/vector handling.

- New core/reputation_intel.py: aggregates AbuseIPDB, AlienVault OTX, VirusTotal and Talos (placeholder) lookups with risk scoring and validation.
- New forensic_examiner skill (instruction + logic): data‑agnostic incident timeline reconstruction using RAG field documentation, search strategy from LLM, and timeline synthesis.
- Config & onboarding updates: load/ask/store external API keys (ABUSEIPDB, ALIENVAULT, VIRUSTOTAL, TALOS) and write them into .env.
- LLM provider: add embedding_dimension property; Ollama caches detected dim, OpenAI returns 1536.
- DB connector: OpenSearch knn_search now applies filters at query level for NMSLIB compatibility; ensure_vector_index default dims changed to 768 and callers encouraged to pass explicit dims.
- RAGEngine: preserve existing vector indexes (do not delete on dim mismatch), detect/choose embedding dimension with sensible fallbacks, and fall back to keyword search when embeddings or KNN fail.
- Chat/router/skills: include conversation history in routing, skill execution, and conversation persistence; format_response implements think/action/reflection + optional anti-hallucination verification and appends queried threat intel sources.
- Add tests and mocks for new functionality and adjust related skill docs/logic.

These changes add optional external TI, improve resilience when embeddings or indices are unavailable/mismatched, and enable a forensic workflow for incident reconstruction.

Remove NetworkBaseliner documentation files

Delete three markdown docs related to NetworkBaseliner v2: NETWORK_BASELINER_BEFORE_AFTER.md, NETWORK_BASELINER_OVERHAUL.md, and NETWORK_BASELINER_v2_SUMMARY.md. These documentation files have been removed (deprecated or consolidated elsewhere).

Rewrite history: Remove config.yaml and SITUATION.md from git tracking

- Removed sensitive files from git history via filter-branch
- config.yaml and SITUATION.md remain locally but are now ignored
- Updated .gitignore to exclude these local-only files
- Updated core/config.py to read credentials from .env
- Added config.yaml.example as clean template
- Updated .env.example with correct variable names
- Added SITUATION.md creation on onboarding in main.py

This ensures secrets never appear in git history or remote repositories.

Improve baseliner/RAG search and routing

Add explicit-only routing for network_baseliner and prevent accidental auto-invocation; chat_router now filters skill routing unless the user explicitly requests the baseliner. Make raw-log search schema-agnostic by adding logs_schema and field_mappings in config.yaml and update rag_querier to use RAGEngine retrieval, return/search terms, and adapt queries to different log schemas (Suricata/ECS/Zeek/etc.). Enhance NetworkBaseliner to detect embedding/index dimension mismatches, load existing baselines before RAG init, pass prior baseline text into LLM prompts so documents are updated rather than blindly replaced, and add helpers to fetch existing baseline docs. Improve combined-context formatting and raw-log summarization (include search terms, geo notes, wider time window). Also update .gitignore (ignore .env and conversations/) and append a new NetworkBaseliner entry to SITUATION.md.
Initial commit

readme_html:
SecurityClaw — Autonomous SOC Agentic Framework

A modular, skill-based autonomous Security Operations Center (SOC) agent that monitors OpenSearch/Elasticsearch data, builds RAG-based behavioral memory, and validates real-time anomalies using LLMs.

Features

  • Skill Modularity — Capabilities as isolated folders with logic.py (Python) + instruction.md (LLM guidance)
  • Heartbeat Loop — Cron-like scheduler: 1-minute anomaly watcher, 6-hour memory builder
  • Provider Agnostic — Swap OpenSearch↔Elasticsearch via config
  • RAG-Based Memory — Vector embeddings stored in OpenSearch; context-aware threat analysis
  • Working Memory — Compact structured memory is stored locally in data/agent_memory.json with bounded sections for investigations, findings, and decisions
  • Conversation-based Investigations — Allows you to investigate threats through an interactive chat interface, with LLM reasoning steps and RAG context retrieval
  • Web Interface — Modern React-based UI for chat, memory visualization, and skill dispatch

Web Interface

Quick Start

0. Prerequisites

  • Python 3.11+ (check with python --version)
  • Git (for cloning the repo)
  • OpenSearch 2.x or Elasticsearch 8.x (or use mock for testing)
  • Ollama (for LLM provider)
  • 4GB+ RAM (recommended for Ollama models; 8GB+ for production)
  • ~2GB disk space for models and vector indices

0.5 Quick Ollama Setup

The current example configuration in config.yaml.example uses:

  • qwen2.5:7b-instruct-q4_K_M for chat/reasoning
  • nomic-embed-text:latest as the lightweight local auxiliary model for embeddings referenced by the sample config

Quick setup:

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh
ollama serve
ollama pull qwen2.5:7b-instruct-q4_K_M
ollama pull nomic-embed-text:latest

1. Create Virtual Environment & Install Dependencies

Step 1a: Clone the repository

git clone https://github.com/SecurityClaw/SecurityClaw.git
cd SecurityClaw

Step 1b: Create a Python virtual environment

# Using venv (built-in)
python3.11 -m venv .venv

# Or using virtualenv (if installed)
virtualenv .venv

Step 1c: Activate the virtual environment

# On Linux/macOS
source .venv/bin/activate

# On Windows (PowerShell)
.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1

# On Windows (Command Prompt)
.venv\Scripts\activate.bat

Step 1d: Install Python dependencies

pip install -r requirements.txt

# Or using Pipenv (if you prefer):
pipenv install --dev

Verify installation:

python -c "import main; import core; print('✓ Dependencies OK')"

2. Interactive Onboarding

.venv/bin/python main.py onboard

The wizard will guide you through:

  • Database: Host, port, SSL, auth
  • LLM: Ollama configuration
  • Connection testing for both services
  • Configuration save to config.yaml and .env

See ONBOARDING.md for details.

3. Start the Agent

.venv/bin/python main.py run

The agent will start a background scheduler and begin polling for anomalies.

4. View Status

In another terminal:

.venv/bin/python main.py status          # Print the compact agent memory snapshot
.venv/bin/python main.py list-skills     # Show loaded skills and intervals
.venv/bin/python main.py dispatch <skill>  # Fire a skill manually (e.g., anomaly_triage)

Architecture

Directory Structure

SecurityClaw/
├── config.yaml                 # Central DB/LLM/RAG configuration
├── .env                        # Secrets (master credentials)
├── main.py                     # CLI entrypoint
│
├── core/
│   ├── config.py              # YAML + env loader
│   ├── memory.py              # Compact structured memory store
│   ├── runner.py              # Conductor (skill discovery, scheduling)
│   ├── scheduler.py           # APScheduler wrapper
│   ├── skill_loader.py        # Dynamic skill discovery
│   ├── db_connector.py        # OpenSearch/ES abstraction
│   ├── llm_provider.py        # Ollama provider
│   └── rag_engine.py          # Embedding store & retrieval
│
├── skills/
│   ├── chat_router/            # API-only: Routes user questions to skills
│   ├── network_baseliner/     # 6h: Aggregate logs → RAG vectors
│   ├── fields_baseliner/      # 1h: Catalog OpenSearch field schemas
│   ├── anomaly_triage/        # Manual: Poll AD findings → enrich → escalate
│   ├── threat_analyst/        # Manual: RAG reasoning → verdict
│   ├── opensearch_querier/    # Manual: Execute database queries
│   ├── forensic_examiner/     # Manual: Build incident timelines
│   ├── baseline_querier/      # Manual: Search behavioral baselines
│   ├── fields_querier/        # Manual: Query field schema catalog
│   ├── geoip_lookup/          # Cron (Tue/Fri 2 AM UTC): Maintain MaxMind DB
│   └── rag_querier/           # Manual: RAG context retrieval
│
├── tests/
│   ├── conftest.py            # Shared fixtures
│   ├── mock_opensearch.py     # In-memory DB (cosine kNN)
│   ├── mock_llm.py            # Deterministic LLM (keyword-dispatched)
│   ├── data_generator.py      # Synthetic network logs & anomalies
│   └── test_*.py              # Offline tests + coverage
│
├── requirements.txt / Pipfile  # Dependencies
└── ONBOARDING.md              # Interactive setup guide

Core Design Principles

Principle Implementation
Skill Modularity Each skill is a folder with logic.py (entrypoint) and instruction.md (LLM system prompt)
Auto-Discovery Runner scans /skills and dynamically loads all valid skills
Stateful Memory A bounded JSON memory store in data/agent_memory.json tracks focus, findings, decisions, and escalation without uncontrolled growth
Scheduled Execution APScheduler fires skills at intervals; intervals defined in skill instruction.md front-matter
Provider Agnostic Abstract BaseDBConnector and BaseLLMProvider allow swapping vendors via config
RAG Context Embeddings stored in vector index; retrieved during LLM analysis for behavioral context
Testability Mock DB, LLM, and data generators enable repeatable offline tests with coverage reporting

Skill Reference

NetworkBaseliner (6-hour cycle)

Purpose: Build baseline of "normal" network behavior.

Logic:

  1. Query recent logs (e.g., last 24 hours)
  2. Aggregate into summaries (typical ports, protocols, byte volumes)
  3. Generate LLM-enhanced descriptions
  4. Store as embedding vectors in the RAG index

Output: Baseline vectors used by ThreatAnalyst for context.

AnomalyTriage (Manual)

Purpose: Poll anomaly detection findings and escalate high-confidence anomalies.

Publication note: This skill is in active validation. Convert to scheduled by adding schedule_interval_seconds: 60 to instruction.md.

Logic:

  1. Query OpenSearch AD index for new findings (cursor-based, from last poll)
  2. Enrich each finding with LLM description (entity, score, severity)
  3. If severity ≥ threshold: write to the escalation queue in agent memory
  4. Update cursor for next poll

Output: Escalated findings in memory, waiting for ThreatAnalyst analysis.

ThreatAnalyst (Manual)

Purpose: Analyze escalated findings using RAG context; issue verdict.

Publication note: This skill is in active validation. Convert to scheduled by adding schedule_interval_seconds: 300 to instruction.md.

Logic:

  1. Read the escalation queue from agent memory
  2. For each finding:
    • Query RAG engine for similar baseline context
    • Build LLM prompt with finding + baseline context
    • Request verdict (TRUE_THREAT, FALSE_POSITIVE, UNKNOWN, ERROR)
  3. Write verdicts and actions to "Recent Decisions"
  4. If TRUE_THREAT: set "Current Focus" and trigger IR playbooks

Output: Verdicts with confidence, MITRE tactic mapping, recommended actions.

GeoIPLookup (weekly refresh + on-demand lookup)

Purpose: Maintain a local MaxMind GeoLite2-City database and answer direct IP geolocation questions.

Logic:

  1. On first use, download the MMDB if missing
  2. Once per week, refresh it if stale
  3. For a supplied IP, return local city / subdivision / country / timezone / coordinate data

Output: Deterministic geolocation fields from the local MaxMind DB.

Publication Status Notes

Skill Status Notes
chat_router Stable Powers web interface and API
network_baseliner Stable Builds behavioral baselines from logs
fields_baseliner Stable Catalogs OpenSearch field schemas
anomaly_triage In-Progress Manual skill; enable scheduling in instruction.md
threat_analyst In-Progress Manual skill; enable scheduling in instruction.md
opensearch_querier Stable Single point of contact for DB queries
forensic_examiner In-Progress Timeline reconstruction; active development
baseline_querier In-Progress Search behavioral baselines; not publication-hardened
fields_querier Stable Search field schema catalog
geoip_lookup Stable MaxMind GeoLite2 maintenance and lookups
rag_querier Stable RAG context retrieval

Legend:

  • Stable: Publication-ready; tested in production patterns
  • In-Progress: Under active validation; feedback welcome
  • Deprecated: Use alternative skill; kept for backwards compatibility

Configuration

config.yaml

agent:
  name: SecurityClaw
  version: "1.0.0"
  skills_dir: skills
  log_level: INFO

scheduler:
  heartbeat_interval_seconds: 60
  memory_build_interval_hours: 6

db:
  provider: opensearch          # or: elasticsearch
  host: localhost
  port: 9200
  use_ssl: false
  verify_certs: false
  username: ""                  # Loaded from .env
  password: ""                  # Loaded from .env
  # Index configuration (configured during onboarding)
  logs_index: securityclaw-logs          # Where to scan for network logs
  anomaly_index: securityclaw-anomalies  # Where AD findings are stored
  vector_index: securityclaw-vectors     # RAG embedding store

llm:
  provider: ollama
  ollama_base_url: http://localhost:11434
  ollama_model: qwen2.5:7b-instruct-q4_K_M
  ollama_embed_model: nomic-embed-text:latest

rag:
  embedding_model: all-MiniLM-L6-v2
  top_k: 5
  similarity_threshold: 0.65

anomaly:
  detector_id: default-detector
  poll_interval_seconds: 60
  severity_threshold: 0.7
  max_findings_per_poll: 50

geoip:
  enabled: true
  db_path: data/geoip/GeoLite2-City.mmdb
  edition_id: GeoLite2-City
  update_interval_days: 7
  download_url: https://download.maxmind.com/app/geoip_download
  timeout_seconds: 60
  license_key: ""               # Loaded from .env via MAXMIND_LICENSE_KEY

Index Configuration Explained

SecurityClaw works with three indices:

Index Purpose Used By Example
logs_index Historical network logs for baseline building NetworkBaseliner (6h cycle) securityclaw-logs, logs-*, filebeat-*
anomaly_index Anomaly Detection results (findings) AnomalyWatcher (1m cycle) securityclaw-anomalies, .opendistro-anomaly-results*
vector_index RAG embeddings (normal behavior baseline) ThreatAnalyst (5m cycle) securityclaw-vectors

Flow:

  1. NetworkBaseliner → queries logs_index → generates summaries → stores embeddings in vector_index
  2. AnomalyWatcher → polls anomaly_index for new findings → escalates to memory
  3. ThreatAnalyst → reads escalations → retrieves context from vector_index → issues verdict

During onboarding, you can use any index names/patterns your environment provides (e.g., if your logs are in filebeat-networking-*, use that instead of securityclaw-logs).

.env (git-ignored)

OPENSEARCH_USERNAME=<your-opensearch-username>
OPENSEARCH_PASSWORD=<your-opensearch-password>
OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434

CLI Commands

# Interactive setup
.venv/bin/python main.py onboard

# Start web interface + backend API + scheduler
.venv/bin/python main.py service

# Run the CLI agent (blocks; press Ctrl+C to stop)
.venv/bin/python main.py run

# Interactive chat in CLI
.venv/bin/python main.py chat

# Fire one skill immediately
.venv/bin/python main.py dispatch anomaly_triage
.venv/bin/python main.py dispatch network_baseliner
.venv/bin/python main.py dispatch threat_analyst

# View working memory
.venv/bin/python main.py status

# List skills and intervals
.venv/bin/python main.py list-skills

# Set logging level
.venv/bin/python main.py --log-level DEBUG run

Web Interface

Starting the Web Server

The web interface provides a modern chat-based UI for interacting with SecurityClaw skills and viewing reasoning steps.

# Activate virtual environment first
source .venv/bin/activate  # or: .venv\Scripts\activate on Windows

# Start the web server + backend API + scheduler
python main.py service

Expected output:

[INFO] Starting web server and API...
[INFO] Frontend available at: http://localhost:3000
[INFO] API backend available at: http://localhost:5000/api
[INFO] Scheduler running in background
[INFO] Press Ctrl+C to stop

Web Interface Features

Chat Interface (http://localhost:3000)

  • Real-time chat: Send questions and receive answers from SecurityClaw skills
  • Reasoning steps: View LLM reasoning and decision-making process
  • Conversation history: Browse and manage past conversations
  • Skill dispatch: Manually trigger skills (anomaly_triage, threat_analyst, etc.)
  • Memory view: Monitor the agent's working memory and findings

API Endpoints (http://localhost:5000/api)

  • GET /api/status — Agent status and memory summary
  • POST /api/chat/stream — Stream chat responses
  • GET /api/conversations — List conversation history
  • DELETE /api/conversations/{id} — Delete a conversation
  • GET /api/skills — List available skills
  • POST /api/skills/{name}/dispatch — Manually trigger a skill
  • GET /api/config (read-only) — View masked configuration

Accessing the Web UI

Local access (single machine):

  • Open your browser to: http://localhost:3000

Remote access (from another machine):

  1. Find the server's IP address:

    hostname -I        # Linux
ipconfig            # Windows
ifconfig            # macOS

  2. Access from remote machine (replace SERVER_IP with actual IP):

    http://SERVER_IP:3000
    • Ensure firewall allows port 3000/5000 traffic
    • For production, use HTTPS and set up reverse proxy (nginx/Apache)

Example: Running Headless (Server Only)

If you only want the API without opening a browser:

# Start server
python main.py service &

# In another terminal, query the API
curl http://localhost:5000/api/status

# Or use the CLI in the main terminal
python main.py dispatch threat_analyst

Troubleshooting Web Server

"Port 3000 already in use"

# Kill the process using port 3000
lsof -ti:3000 | xargs kill -9     # Linux/macOS
netstat -ano | findstr :3000       # Windows

"Cannot connect to API"

  • Verify backend is running: curl http://localhost:5000/api/status
  • Check firewall rules
  • Look for errors in the main terminal

"Chat not responding"

  • Check LLM availability (Ollama running?)
  • View logs: python main.py --log-level DEBUG service
  • Check config.yaml for correct provider settings

Testing

All tests are offline by default (mock DB + mock LLM) and now emit coverage reports via pytest-cov.

# Run the full suite with coverage
.venv/bin/python -m pytest

# Run a specific test file
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_rag.py -v

# Optional HTML coverage report
.venv/bin/python -m pytest --cov-report=html

Coverage XML is written to coverage.xml for CI/reporting.

Current publication-prep baseline: the full suite is measured automatically, but aggregate coverage is still dragged down by in-progress modules and provider-specific adapters. Treat the report as a measurement tool, not as a claim that every skill is publication-hardened.

What's Tested

Layer Tests Notes
Config (via conftest) YAML + env loading
Scheduler 13 Job registration, dispatch, intervals, cron expressions
DB Abstraction 20 Search, kNN, anomaly findings, bulk indexing
LLM Abstraction 11 Embedding, chat, canned responses
RAG Engine 15 Store, retrieve, context building, category filters
Skill Loader 14 Discovery, instruction loading, interval parsing
Skills active coverage Stable orchestration paths are covered; in-progress skills remain under active validation
Data Generator 24 Synthetic logs, anomalies, baseline chunks, embeddings

Redundant supervisor routing tests were consolidated to keep the publication suite smaller and easier to maintain.

Writing a New Skill

Anatomy of a Skill

skills/my_skill/
├── logic.py          # Python
└── instruction.md    # LLM guidance

logic.py:

"""
skills/my_skill/logic.py

Context dict keys:
  - db        → BaseDBConnector
  - llm       → BaseLLMProvider
  - memory    → AgentMemory
  - config    → Config
  - skills    → dict of loaded Skill objects
"""
from pathlib import Path

SKILL_NAME = "my_skill"
INSTRUCTION_PATH = Path(__file__).parent / "instruction.md"

def run(context: dict) -> dict:
    """
    Main entry point. Called by Runner on schedule.
    
    Return a dict with status, results, etc.
    """
    db = context.get("db")
    llm = context.get("llm")
    memory = context.get("memory")
    config = context.get("config")
    
    # Your logic here
    memory.add_finding("Found something interesting")
    
    return {
        "status": "ok",
        "findings": 5,
    }

instruction.md:

---
schedule_interval_seconds: 300
---

# My Skill

You are a security analyst specializing in [X].

When given anomalies, your job is to:
1. [Step 1]
2. [Step 2]

Respond in JSON format with:
```json
{
  "verdict": "...",
  "confidence": ...,
  "reasoning": "..."
}


---

## Extending SecurityClaw

### Add a New Skill

1. Create `skills/my_skill/` directory
2. Write `logic.py` with `run(context)` function
3. Write `instruction.md` with LLM guidance and optional `schedule_interval_seconds`
4. Restart agent or run `.venv/bin/python main.py dispatch my_skill` to test

### Add a DB Backend

1. Subclass `BaseDBConnector` in `core/db_connector.py`
2. Set `db.provider: my_db` in `config.yaml`
3. Update `build_db_connector()` factory to instantiate your class

### Add an LLM Backend

1. Subclass `BaseLLMProvider` in `core/llm_provider.py`
2. Set `llm.provider: my_llm` in `config.yaml`
3. Update `build_llm_provider()` factory to instantiate your class

---

## Troubleshooting

**"Module 'X' not found"**
```bash
.venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt

"Cannot connect to OpenSearch"

  • Verify OpenSearch is running: curl -u admin:admin http://localhost:9200
  • Check config.yaml host/port
  • Check firewall rules

"Cannot connect to Ollama"

  • Start Ollama: ollama serve
  • Pull the sample models: ollama pull qwen2.5:7b-instruct-q4_K_M && ollama pull nomic-embed-text:latest
  • Verify base URL in config.yaml

"Skill not loading"

  • Check /skills/<name>/logic.py exists
  • Verify run(context) function signature
  • Check logs: .venv/bin/python main.py --log-level DEBUG run

"No findings detected"

  • Seed mock DB: See tests/conftest.py for example synthetic data
  • Check anomaly indices: curl http://localhost:9200/_cat/indices?v
  • Verify detector ID in config.yaml

Performance Notes

  • LLM Calls: Each anomaly watcher and threat analyst cycle calls the LLM 1+ times (Ollama: ~1s per call)
  • RAG Retrieval: kNN search is O(n) in mock; ~1ms per query on seeded DB
  • Scheduler: Background APScheduler has minimal overhead (~1% CPU idle)

Support

Security / Publication Checklist

  • config.yaml, .env, and data/agent_memory.json are intended to stay local.

  • Use config.yaml.example as the public template.

  • Run a quick scan before publishing:

    git grep -nEI '(password|api[-]?key|BEGIN [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY|sk-)' -- . git log --all -G 'password|api[-]?key|sk-' --oneline
